name: Circle K International
summary: Circle K International is the oldest student-led collegiate service organization. We operate on our tenants Service, Leadership & Fellowship to develop ourselves into young professionals and value being a servant leader within our community.

name: Financial Management Association, UNLV
summary: The UNLV Financial Management Association is a non-profit student organization offering support and development to students interested in the field of Finance.

name: American Marketing Association, UNLV
summary: The UNLV Student Chapter of the American Marketing Association (AMA) provides students of all majors the opportunity to network and interact with professionals, as well as incorporate practical application of business and marketing principles.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
summary: The BGSA is an organization that promotes the empowerment, professional development, and scholarship of students of color navigating through higher learning. It has an expectation of excellence in students within-and outside-the university culture.

name: University Association of Social Work Students
summary: UASWS strives to cultivate connections between students and professionals within the social work field, both at UNLV and within the community through special events, volunteering and empowerment, as well as social activities.

name: UNLV Debate Team
summary: The UNLV Debate Team represents UNLV at intercollegiate policy debate tournaments at universities across America.  The team is dedicated to competitive excellence and campus and community outreach to promote debate and informed civic discourse.

name: UNLV Collegiate DECA
summary: DECA prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in colleges and universities around the globe.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
summary: Zeta, Tau, Alpha, ZTA, Think Pink, Breast Cancer Awareness and Education, female fraternity, leadership, sisterhood, social, scholarship

name: Lee Business School Ambassadors
summary: Lee Business School Ambassadors, Ambassadors, Lee Business School, LBS, UNLV, Academics, Business

name: American Society of Landscape Architects, UNLV Student Chapter
summary: Landscape architecture embodies the art and science of design, planning, and management of the land. Through our education and advocacy, we aim to create a sustainable living environment and make a positive impact on our surroundings and society.

name: UNLV German Club
summary: The UNLV German Club brings together a diverse group of students with a shared interest in the language and culture of German-speaking countries, hosting a weekly conversation table and organizing culturally themed events throughout each semester.

name: Psychology Clinical Student Committee
summary: psychology, graduate student, clinical

name: Residence Hall Association, UNLV
summary: The UNLV Residence Hall Association (RHA) plans fun activities for multiple identities of students living on campus, advocates on behalf of residents to administration and creates stronger community ties through leadership conferences.

name: MIS Society
summary: MIS Society is a student organization that offers professional development opportunities to graduate and undergraduate students interested in Management Information Systems and related technologies.

name: Rebel Vets Organization
summary: We are an organization that welcomes all members who are veterans, active duty, spouses, guardsmen, dependents, and civilians. We are a community that supports each other, strengthens community ties, and assists with future endeavors.

name: Tau Beta Pi
summary: Tau Beta Pi, TBP, The Engineering Honor Society - Nevada Beta

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
summary: We are a team of students who design, build, test, and compete a baja off-roading car in the international Baja SAE collegiate series. All while learning the hands-on application of engineering and how to work in a team atmosphere.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), is a  nonprofit organization that is owned and managed by its members. With more than 24,000 members around the world, NSBE is one of the largest student-governed organizations.

name: National Society of Minorities in Hospitality
summary:
NSMH is the premiere professional organization for hospitality students.

We are an organization of students and professionals representing an array of social and cultural backgrounds and a wealth of unique perspectives and ideas.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
summary: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, international student-run organization dedicated to providing unmatched programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architectural education.

name: Engineers Without Borders
summary: Engineers Without Borders USA (EWB-USA) builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs and equip leaders to solve the world’s most pressing challenges.

summary: The American Society of Civil Engineer UNLV Student Chapter is one of the largest student organizations in the College of Engineering. We are known around campus as the ones who build the legendary Concrete Canoes!

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. Alpha Epsilon Omicron Chapter
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college consisting of over 750 chapters nationwide.

name: UNLV Anthropology Society
summary: A student organization aimed at enhancing communication and interaction among students interested in anthropology.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
summary: Hello! If you are interested in joining AED (the only pre-health honor society at UNLV), please contact our advisor, Dr. Bubb at Dan.Bubb@unlv.edu. After he verifies your GPA (3.3 minimum requirement) we will schedule your new member orientation!

name: Black Student Organization
summary: Our Black Student Organization is a social political organization that aims to support the black diaspora through cultural enrichment, volunteerism and advocacy for students.

name: Graduate & Professional Student Association
summary: The Graduate Professional Student Association is the recognized governing body serving the currently enrolled and admitted UNLV graduate and professional student population.

name: Association of Environmental and Engineering Geologists
summary: Geology, Engineering Geology, Engineering, Environmental Geology

name: UNLV Young Democrats
summary: The official Young Democrats/College Democrats organization at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
summary: We are an Accounting, Finance, and Information Systems honor society aimed for preparing future professionals and leaders in the world through professional development, service, and leadership.

name: Phi Alpha Theta, Psi Sigma Chapter
summary: academic, history, liberal arts

name: American Choral Director's Association UNLV Student Chapter
summary: Choral conducting, singing, music education.

summary: The Student Organization and Resource Center is located in room 305 of the Student Union. We support student organizations in their programs as well as assist students who want to get involved find organizations they may be interested in.

name: UNLV Student Affiliates in School Psychology
summary: UNLV SASP is a community for graduate students affiliated with the discipline of and related to school psychology. We strive to build networks, educate and foster understanding of the field, and develop leaders to strengthen the discipline in the future.

summary: Students joining HFTP UNLV can benefit from the extraordinary networking opportunities, industry-leading certification programs and events, and essential resources for professional growth offered to all HFTP members.

summary: Neon Lit Reading Series is a monthly event featuring writers from UNLV's graduate Creative Writing programs (MFA and PhD), and invited guests.

summary: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications.

summary: American Society of Interior Designers is a student-run organization dedicated to connecting, mentoring and preparing students to enter the professional world.

The Rebel Venture Fund is UNLV's very own student-led venture capital investment group that
invests capital in startups and early stage high-growth companies.

summary: National, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service. Immense opportunities for scholarships, networking, and professional development available.

summary: Dedicated to Empower Women in the Law

AISES: Advancing Indigenous people in STEM studies and careers every day.

Is your major in engineering, gaming, hospitality, technology, math-related or any of the sciences? Join us! 
We are building a UNLV chapter of AISES.

name: UNLV Association for Computing Machinery

summary: The Neuroscience Undergraduate Research Organization (NURO) enhances student experiences at UNLV by offering professional development, research opportunities, and community involvement through workshops, resource coordination, and outreach.

summary: Armenian Student Association is an organization dedicated to bringing together Armenian students in the community and promoting Armenian culture and language at UNLV.

summary: Student Affairs Strategic Planning & Assessment provides faculty and staff in Student Affairs departments with leadership and support for strategic planning, program review, and assessment processes.

summary: The Office of the Vice Provost for Undergraduate Education is comprised of general education, online education, academic program assessment, advising practice, and retention, progression, and completion initiatives.

name: UNLV Rebel Robotics
summary: UNLV Robotics is dedicated to improving the local robotics community by competing in various robotics competitions, specifically VEX U, in order to educate and improve member’s abilities in multiple engineering disciplines.

name: Honors College
summary: Honors College exists to channel the energies, refine the ideas, and ignite the passions of our remarkable students.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America, University of Nevada Las Vegas Chapter
summary: YDSA is the youth and student section of the Democratic Socialists of America. We are students organizing to fight for the immediate needs of workers and students while building our capacity to fight for more radical and structural changes.

name: Rebel Transfer Student Organization
summary: "Helping Transfer Students Bridge The Gap To The Rebel Family." Aims to be Resource for the 2000+ Transfer students each semester. Join our Discord to stay up-to-date with Transfer News: https://discord.gg/MpQZYaFs5C

summary: The Student Organization of Latinxs strives to educate, inform, and empower Latinx students to succeed in their UNLV careers while celebrating and sharing our diverse cultures.

summary: We strive to provide responsive academic, social, and administrative support to student veterans, as well as active-duty military members and dependents using VA education benefits.

name: University Police Services, Southern Command
summary: UPD is the full-service police department for the UNLV, CSN, NSC, and DRI campuses.

name: UNLV Comadres
summary: UNLV Comadres is an interdisciplinary Latinx graduate student organization open to all graduate students who identify as Latinx mujeres and nonbinary Latinxs attending the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

summary: As a community , the LGSA vision is to create a welcoming community to all Latinx and Afro-latinx graduate students, where they feel supported in their professional aspirations and engaged (included) in our community.

name: UNLV College of Fine Arts
summary: Boldly launching visionaries who transform the global community through collaboration, scholarship, and innovation.

name: College of Education Student Services Center
summary: The ESSC provides academic advising, career development and student success services, and field placement support for Education & Human Services majors.

name: Office of Information Technology
summary: OIT manages the campus technology students use, including computer labs, wireless, ACE account, and more.

name: From the Brush Arts and Literary Journal
summary: The From the Brush Arts and Literary Journal is a student-led organization established by UNLV's Honors College. Our mission is to provide an enriching outlet for honors students nationwide to have their creative works professionally recognized.

name: Arnold Air Society - Gen Wilbur Creech Squadron
summary: Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary, service organization advocating the support of aerospace power, that helps enhancing the development of future Air Force and Space Force officers while promoting community service & professional development

name: College Republicans
summary:
The official College Republicans chapter at UNLV, forging leaders since 2022.

We foster the moral and patriotic leaders of tomorrow at UNLV, one student at a time.

name: The Literate Earth Project at UNLV
summary: The Literate Earth Project (LEP) is a non-profit organization that aims to promote literacy and provide access to educational resources to underprivileged communities around the world. We collect books and raise money to build libraries in Uganda.

name: Pre-Law Women of UNLV
summary: The Pre-Law Women of UNLV serves as a community of women pursuing a career in the legal profession. PLW offers support, career development, networking, and encouragement for all types of women looking to connect with other legal-minded women.

name: LEE Business Connections
summary: A place for incoming and current Lee Business students to connect with one another.

name: Rebel Start-Up
summary: RSU aims to provide opportunities for students to share their entrepreneurial ideas and convert them into reality through mentorship, networking, collaboration, competition, and experience.

name: Associated General Contractors - Student Chapter
summary: Our student chapter serves as the hub for students to connect, learn, and collaborate with industry experts. We aim to bridge the gab between academic theory and practical experiences preparing our students for a successful career in construction.

name: Rebels R.I.S.E.
summary: The Rebels R.I.S.E. scholar program provides annual cohorts of UNLV undergraduate students with opportunities to encourage meaningful learning experiences for students during their time at UNLV.

name: Collegiate National Association for Music Educators
summary: A collegiate music education organization that provides professional development, promotes advocacy and serves the community.  Any music education major can join.

name: Color Business Association
summary: The Color Business Association (CBA) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, founded in 2022, is dedicated to connecting a diverse group of students to the corporate world through personalized professional development, workshops, and networking events.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: The Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) strives to promote Asian Pacific American topics and to contribute to the multicultural awareness at the William S. Boyd School of Law.

name: Council for Exceptional Children, University of Nevada, Las Vegas Chapter
summary: Council for Exceptional Children is a dedicated student community advancing the success of individuals with disabilities & gifted talents. We equip current and future educators to lead through advocacy and networking.

name: Environmental Law Society
summary: The Environmental Law Society is a supportive community for law students interested in protecting the environment and pursuing careers in environmental & natural resources law. ELS raises awareness about current environmental law and policy issues.

summary: UNLV SIAM (Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics) serves as an organization for STEM enthusiasts of all levels, with emphasis in math and professional development.

name: Dispute Resolution Society
summary: The Dispute Resolution Society is one of the oldest student organizations at UNLV and Boyd School of Law. We organize events focused on Alternative Dispute Resolution in the community and in concert with the Saltman Center for Conflict Resolution.

name: Music Teachers National Association
summary: MTNA is an organization of musicians which seeks to:  (1) engage the public, (2) sustain the profession, and (3) inspire its members. At all levels, MTNA is devoted to nurturing the musical community and raising the standards of music education.

name: Armenian Law Students Association
summary: The Armenian Law Students Association's mission is to foster goodwill among our members, assist and encourage each other in pursuing our legal education and careers, and generate interest in Armenian issues and culture.

name: UNLV Jazz Education Network Collegiate Chapter
summary: The UNLV Jazz Education Network Collegiate Chapter is dedicated to promoting: jazz pedagogy, performance and expanding the jazz audience.

name: Cyber Clinic
summary: The Cyber Clinic strives to protect and educate small businesses while building the foundation for tomorrow’s cybersecurity professionals.

name: UNLV Collegiate 100
summary: The primary purpose of the Collegiate 100® program is to provide an avenue for 100 Black Men of America, Inc. chapters to continue their one-on-one and group mentoring to students as they matriculate from high school to college.

name: La Voz, Latino Law Student Association
summary: La Voz is the Latino Law Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law. Our mission is to empower and support traditionally underrepresented students, aiming to increase representation in the legal profession.

name: UNLV OUTLaws
summary: Serving and supporting LGBTQIA+ and allied students at the William S. Boyd School of Law and beyond.

name: International Student Organization
summary: The International Student Organization(ISO) connects international students, fostering a welcoming & inclusive environment. Promoting cultural exchange & understanding through events dedicated to creating a sense of community among international Students

name: Minority Serving Institution Student Council
summary: The MSISC is taking steps to ensure student-facing programs, service models, and policies exist to help historically underrepresented students thrive at UNLV.

name: If/When/How - Lawyering for Reproductive Justice
summary: Fighting for people to have the power to determine if, when, and how to actualize sexual and reproductive wellbeing on their own terms.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
summary: Gamma Iota Sigma is an international professional fraternity with 100 Chapters Nationwide with 5,000+ members organized to promote, encourage, and sustain student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial science as professions.

name: Transfer Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law
summary: The Transfer Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law is an organization made to help transfer students integrate into their new environment.

name: Child Care Access Means Parents In School
summary: This organization is for student participants in the Child Care Access Means Parents in School (CCAMPIS) program to support students that are parents toward successful degree completion.

name: Women in Mathematics
summary: Women in Mathematics empowers and advances students in mathematics at UNLV.

name: Screen Acting Club
summary: Screen Acting Club is a way to explore different creative techniques. The essence of SAC is to work with or without experience in a fun environment. This club is a safe place, no pressure only instinct. How you talk/interact, is the dependent.

name: SGI Buddhism
summary: Soka Gakkai International (SGI) was founded on January 26, 1975, as a worldwide network of socially engaged Buddhists dedicated to a common vision of a better world through the empowerment of the individuals through Buddhist Humanism.

name: Undergraduate Student Association of Research
summary: U-STAR is a student-led support network for undergraduates interested in or currently participating in research at UNLV.

name: UNLV Grad Pride Alliance
summary: UNLV Grad Pride Alliance, or GPA works to educate fellow Graduate students about LGBTQIA+ experiences, encourage current and future professional and personal allyship while creating a social space for LGBTQIA+ grad students.

name: Business In Action
summary: Business in Action is a philanthropic student organization within Lee Business School that hosts outreach events each semester partnering with other student organizations on campus and within the community to make a difference for those in need.

name: Corporate Law Society
summary: Corporate Law Society's mission is to inform and educate law students about corporate law and its diverse work opportunities, as well as help network students with attorneys and law firms in and outside of Las Vegas.

name: WiCyS Student Chapter
summary: Women in Cybersecurity (WiCyS) is a community that promotes women’s education, participation, and leadership in the field of cybersecurity.

name: William S. Boyd School of Law
summary: The William S. Boyd School of Law is the only Law School in the state of Nevada.

name: UNLV Rebel Media Group
summary: Rebel Media Group is a full-service digital media agency located in UNLV’s Greenspun Hall.

name: Fostering Scholars
summary: Support program for students who have experienced foster care after the age of 13 while at UNLV

name: Youth Voice of Nevada
summary: The Youth Voice of Nevada is to empower and inspire young people to become active and engaged members of their communities, and to provide a platform for youth to have their voices heard and to make a positive impact on their community

name: UNLV American Indigenous Business Leaders
summary: The mission of AIBL is to increase the representation of American Indians and Alaska Natives in business and entrepreneurial ventures through education and leadership development opportunities.

name: Bridge Las Vegas
summary: Bridge Las Vegas aims to reduce political polarization by fostering healthy and positive conversations and helping promote strong communication and open-mindedness in both politics and other topics of discussion.

name: Club Management Association of America UNLV Student Chapter
summary: UNLV CMAA is for UNLV Hospitality students looking to network and learn about the private club industry. We host club tours at various membership-based clubs, host guest speakers, and industry panels.

name: Native American Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of the Native American Law Students Association is to articulate and promote the professional needs and goals of Native American law students and their allies in Native American law pursuing a legal education at the Boyd School of Law.

name: UNLV Battery Workforce Challenge
summary: The UNLV & CSN Battery Workforce Challenge is a collaborative program designed to cultivate a supportive and engaging learning environment for students seeking careers in the rapidly growing electric vehicle battery industry.

name: College of Education Alumni Engagement
summary: We offer programming and services to COE alumni, students, faculty, staff, and friends. #rebelsforever #unlvcoealumni

name: Generative Artificial Intelligence and Legal Technology Club
summary: Generative AI and Legal Technology Club is a student organization formed under the auspices of the UNLV William S. Boyd School of Law, dedicated to exploring the intersection of artificial intelligence, legal technology.

name: UNLV Music Business Club
summary: UMBC aims to provide a space for musicians on campus to learn, network, and collaborate through guest speakers and workshops, as well as opportunities to show their talent through songwriting and production contests.

name: Latinx Faculty and Staff Alliance
summary: An affinity group for Latino/a/x and Hispanic Faculty and Staff at UNLV.

name: UNLV Wind Orchestra to France 2025
summary: This organization's primary goal is to raise and obtain funding for the UNLV Wind Orchestra's 2025 France trip.

name: Student Organization for Learning and Research
summary: Graduate student organization supporting Master's, Doctoral, and undergraduate students enrolled or interested in Educational Psychology, Learning Sciences, Higher Education, and other degree programs within the College of Education.

name: Actuarial Science Club
summary: A place for STEM students to get together and study, to meet people who share the same interest's. Love all things STEM. (OPEN TO ALL)

name: Narcare
summary: Narcare is a student organization that is dedicated to community empowerment to address the opioid crisis and substance abuse in the Las Vegas area. We aim to provide education for students about effective harm reduction tools and Narcan use.

name: UNLV Gaming Law Journal
summary: GLJ is published by the students of Boyd School of Law at UNLV in conjunction with the International Masters of Gaming Law.

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
summary: IPLS aims to empower future legal professionals by providing resources and opportunities to engage with key issues and innovations shaping the field.

name: Division of Research
summary: The Division of Research is committed to supporting and promoting a culture of excellence and integrity in research and creative/scholarly activity that benefits the university, community, and state.

name: Student Life
summary: Student Life includes programs and facilities that focus on the student experience — housing, recreation, student union, student diversity, student activities and organizations. It also has a Business Services Office.

name: Hospitality Themed Housing Community
summary: It is a community of peers who have some of the same courses or who assist each other in studying for courses they have previously taken. Participants interact with faculty members in the hospitality college through programs that include mentorship

name: Engineering Thematic Housing Community
summary: In partnership with the Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering Floor, this community has its very own computer lab outfitted with commonly-used software programs, such as CAD and C++, which can be accessed at any time.

name: Stonewall Suites Thematic Housing Community
summary: This community is designed to provide a safe, comfortable, and educational living and learning environment for LGBTQ+ students and their allies as well as being an inclusive environment for students who identify as any gender.

name: Recovery Evolved: Validating Individual Values and Experiences
summary: This RSO intends to create a supportive community for students who are interested in or are working on changing, reducing, or moderating their substance use and unwanted habits.

name: Policy and Legislation Society
summary: Our main mission is to support our members (law students) in the development of their legal careers and to provide opportunity in Policy, Law, Lobbying and Legislation. PALS plans, coordinates and organizes the annual making the law competition.

name: UNLV Association of Black Journalists
summary: The National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ) is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that provides innovative, quality programs and services to its members and advocates on behalf of Black journalists and media professionals in the U.S. and worldwide

name: African-Americans In Gaming
summary: Provide a sense of community, support and opportunity for African Americans in the gaming industry.

name: The American Concrete Institute
summary: ACI is an international organization with networking and technical opportunities for students and professionals working with Concrete and related technologies. Civil and construction majors are encouraged to join us, but all concrete lovers are welcome!

name: Campus Thrifts
summary: Campus Thrifts is a student-run thrift club that brings the second hand community to our college campus. Sustainable and high quality clothing is important. We have info-sessions, thrift meetups, and community involvement!

name: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club
summary: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club fosters dialogue on banned, challenged, and censored books. We champion intellectual freedom, amplify marginalized voices, and explore the cultural impact of literary censorship through events and discussion.

name: Black Social Workers Society
summary: The Black Social Workers Society aims to connect and elevate students, particularly of the African diaspora, who are interested in social work and social justice. We provide opportunities for education, career preparation, service, and social connection.

name: The Hospitality Collective
summary: A student-driven community dedicated to learning, collaboration, and innovation in hospitality—connecting with industry professionals to inspire excellence, foster growth, and shape the future of the industry.

name: Raising Our AANHPI Rights
summary: ROAR (Raising Our AANHPI Rights) dedicates its mission to empowering students with the leadership opportunities and social networking required to give their cultural heritage a voice in UNLV and Nevada affairs. Join us on our bi-weekly meetings!

name: The Forward Party at UNLV
summary: The Forward Party at UNLV is a nonpartisan student organization committed to fixing our broken political system through collaboration, civic engagement, independent media, and local action.

name: Rebel Career Ambassadors
summary: Rebel Career Ambassadors aims to prepare students for the workforce by offering career readiness training, peer support, and networking opportunities with professionals, employers, and alumni. We foster a supportive space for all.

name: Business Law Association of UNLV
summary: The Business Law Association (BLA) at UNLV builds a community for undergrads interested in law and business, connecting them to resources, professionals, and networks to help them achieve academic and career success.”

name: The Lee Advisory Board
summary: The Lee Business School Advisory Board (LAB) connects student organizations, college leadership, and alumni to foster collaboration, leadership, and engagement across the undergraduate business community.

name: Pocket Battle Bots
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to promote hands-on engineering, creative
design, and friendly competition through the construction and battling of small scale
3D printed combat robots to compete in sanctioned battles in an enclosed arena.

name: Alpha Lambda Delta
summary: Alpha Lambda Delta is a national honor society recognizing academic excellence in first-year college students. With 280+ chapters and 1M+ initiates, ALD promotes scholarship, leadership, and meaningful career growth.

name: ACM SIGGRAPH @ UNLV
summary: A student-led ACM SIGGRAPH chapter focused on exploring computer graphics and interactive technologies through research, collaboration, and creative practice, fostering innovation and community engagement in visual computing.

name: Rebels for Randi
summary: Rebels for Randi is an organization for all UNLV students to come together in hopes of creating a new UNLV mascot, Randi the Red Rock. Through our support of Randi, we will support each other’s personal and professional development so that we can all bec

name: National Association of Hispanic Journalists @ UNLV
summary: NAHJ @ UNLV supports Latino students pursuing a career in media. Members will sharpen their professional life by working in a team and getting a hands-on experience in the journalism field.

name: Womxn of Color Collective - Las Vegas
summary: The Womxn of Color Collective is dedicated to creating a safe space and a community within the William S. Boyd School of Law where the unique experiences, opinions, challenges, and successes of womxn and womxn of color are shared.

name: Flintknapping Club
summary: A relaxed club to practice hands-on methods of stone tool manufacture with current and past technologies.

name: Howell Town Theme Community
summary: Howell Town aims to create an environment that focuses on the history, prosperity, nad enrich ment of the African and Black Diaspora communities.

name: UNLV Student Athlete Advisory Commitee
summary: The UNLV Student-Athlete Advisory Committee is dedicated to fostering open communication between student-athletes, campus and athletics administration, ensuring the timely dissemination of information and providing meaningful feedback to athletes.

name: Lōkahi Theme Community
summary: Welcome to all, this community is dedicated to honoring, celebrating, and sharing the Pacific Islands’ rich heritage with pride to foster a united, supportive, and inclusive environment for those who are close and far from home.

name: Ascend UNLV
summary: Ascend is a business student organization that promotes professional development, leadership, and community. Guided by values of unity, hard work, and optimism. Ascend is open and welcoming to students of all backgrounds to connect and socialize!

name: South Asian Law Student Association
summary: SALSA seeks to strengthen the rapidly growing South Asian legal community in Las Vegas with a recognized and trusted forum for professional growth and advancement that promotes civil rights and access to justice within the South Asian community.
